root_text.sn48.14:1.5: Imesaṁ kho, bhikkhave, pañcannaṁ indriyānaṁ samattā paripūrattā arahaṁ hoti, tato mudutarehi anāgāmī hoti, tato mudutarehi sakadāgāmī hoti, tato mudutarehi sotāpanno hoti, tato mudutarehi dhammānusārī hoti, tato mudutarehi saddhānusārī hoti.
root_text.sn48.14:1.6: Iti kho, bhikkhave, paripūraṁ paripūrakārī ārādheti, padesaṁ padesakārī ārādheti.

translation_text.sn48.14:1.3: The faculties of faith, energy, mindfulness, immersion, and wisdom.
translation_text.sn48.14:1.5: Someone who has completed and fulfilled these five faculties is a perfected one. If they are weaker than that, they’re a non-returner … a once-returner … a stream-enterer … a follower of teachings … a follower by faith.
translation_text.sn48.14:1.6: So, mendicants, if you practice partially you succeed partially. If you practice fully you succeed fully.
translation_text.sn48.14:1.7: But in any case, these five faculties are not a waste, I say.”
